summary: Russian drone and missile attacks have intensified, striking fuel infrastructure in Trostyanets, Naftogaz facilities in Poltava and Kharkiv, and causing multiple casualties in Okhtyrka, Zaporizhzhia and Dnipropetrovsk regions. Ukrainian forces reported successful SBU strikes on the Vtorovo oil‑product pipeline station in Vladimir oblast, disrupting Russian logistics. Civilian hardships are growing, with Russian fuel queues and reports of up to 16,000 Ukrainian civilians held in Russian captivity.
key_points: Gas station network in Trostyanets was completely destroyed, with similar attacks reported in Kharkiv and Zaporizhzhia (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31213).
Russian forces launched missile and drone strikes on Naftogaz facilities in Poltava and Kharkiv regions over two days (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31211).
Russian drones conducted a mass attack on Okhtyrka district, hitting private houses and fuel stations, injuring 10 people (Source: @UkraineNow/82219).
SBU carried out successful strikes on the Vtorovo oil‑product pipeline station in Vladimir oblast, a key logistics node for Russian fuel exports (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31213).
Attacks on Zaporizhzhia and Kharkiv AFS caused a gas tank fire, building damage and destruction of 10 pieces of equipment (Source: @UkraineNow/82222).
More than 40 attacks were reported in Dnipropetrovsk region, resulting in one death, injuries, damaged fuel stations and fires on a wheat field (Source: @UkraineNow/82229).
Russian civilians are queuing for fuel overnight as a result of Ukrainian attacks on Russian fuel infrastructure (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31213).
Ukrainian officials estimate up to 16,000 Ukrainian civilians are being held in Russian captivity (Source: @UkraineNow/82224).

sections.executive_summary: Russian drone and missile attacks have intensified, striking fuel infrastructure in Trostyanets, Naftogaz facilities in Poltava and Kharkiv, and causing multiple casualties in Okhtyrka, Zaporizhzhia and Dnipropetrovsk regions. Ukrainian forces reported successful SBU strikes on the Vtorovo oil‑product pipeline station in Vladimir oblast, disrupting Russian logistics. Civilian hardships are growing, with Russian fuel queues and reports of up to 16,000 Ukrainian civilians held in Russian captivity.

points: Gas station network in Trostyanets was completely destroyed, with similar attacks reported in Kharkiv and Zaporizhzhia, highlighting vulnerability of civilian fuel infrastructure (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31213).

Over the past two days, Russian missile and drone strikes targeted Naftogaz facilities in Poltava and Kharkiv, affecting energy supply and potentially causing operational disruptions (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31211).
Mass drone attack on Okhtyrka district hit private houses and fuel stations, resulting in 10 injuries and complicating local defense efforts (Source: @UkraineNow/82219).
SBU successfully struck the Vtorovo oil‑product pipeline station in Vladimir oblast, a key node for Russian fuel export logistics, indicating deep‑strike capability (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31213).

Attacks on Zaporizhzhia and Kharkiv AFS caused a gas tank fire, building damage and destruction of 10 pieces of equipment, showing continued pressure on Russian rear areas (Source: @UkraineNow/82222).

More than 40 attacks in Dnipropetrovsk region caused one death, injuries, damaged fuel stations, a fire in a private house and a wheat field, reflecting sustained Ukrainian offensive activity (Source: @UkraineNow/82229).
Russian civilians are queuing for fuel overnight in multiple cities, reflecting the impact of Ukrainian attacks on Russian fuel supply chains (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31213).
Reports indicate up to 16,000 Ukrainian civilians are being held in Russian captivity, a significant humanitarian concern (Source: @UkraineNow/82224).
Russian authorities warned of possible Iskander‑M ballistic missile launches from Bryansk and Kursk regions, though no launches were confirmed (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68145, @mon1tor_ua/68115).
Russian air defense claimed to have shot down 124 Ukrainian drones over Russian regions and the Black Sea on a single day (Source: @tass_agency/382692).
Russian military aviation adjusted schedules due to restrictions at Sochi airport, indicating operational constraints (Source: @tass_agency/382722).
Two Colombian mercenaries serving with Ukrainian forces were captured by Russian troops, highlighting foreign fighter involvement (Source: @tass_agency/382713).
Ukrainian drone activity was reported from Sumy heading toward Poltava, and from Kharkiv toward Staryi Merchyk, indicating pressure on eastern supply routes (Source: @kpszsu/66629, @kpszsu/66596).
A reactive UAV was observed over Chernihiv heading toward Mena, suggesting reconnaissance or strike missions in the north‑eastern sector (Source: @kpszsu/66609).
Ukrainian forces reported a cluster of UAVs from Kharkiv heading to Staryi Merchyk, potentially targeting logistics hubs (Source: @kpszsu/66596).
Multiple UAVs were noted over Sumy and Poltava regions, reflecting continued aerial contest over eastern oblasts (Source: @kpszsu/66629, @kpszsu/66556).

Ukrainian intelligence warned of possible large‑scale ballistic missile attacks on Kyiv, with bomber redeployments from the Ukrainka airfield, indicating strategic threat from the east (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68111).
SBU strikes on the Vtorovo station in Vladimir oblast demonstrate Ukrainian reach into Russian interior, affecting eastern logistics (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31213).
Reactive UAVs were reported over Kyiv region moving westward, indicating potential threats to the capital from the north (Source: @kpszsu/66627).
Ukrainian sources warned of ballistic missile threats targeting Kyiv from Kursk and Bryansk regions, heightening northern defensive alerts (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68145, @mon1tor_ua/68115).
Ukrainian UAV activity was noted over Chernihiv heading toward Mena, suggesting northern reconnaissance (Source: @kpszsu/66609).
Two circular radar alerts indicated possible missile approaches toward Kyiv, though details were not confirmed (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68143).
Ukrainian intelligence reported preparations for a major attack on Kyiv involving various missile types, though the timing was unspecified (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68111).
Ukrainian UAVs were observed heading toward Borispil via Brovary district, indicating pressure on northern air corridors (Source: @eRadarrua/98405).
Ukrainian drones attacked Zaporizhzhia, igniting a gas tank and damaging an administrative building, showing continued pressure on southern energy assets (Source: @UkraineNow/82222).
Drone strikes in Kharkiv region damaged an AFS building and caused a 400 m² fire, highlighting southern rear‑area vulnerability (Source: @UkraineNow/82222).
Ukrainian forces reported six injured, including a child, after a night attack on Zaporizhzhia with three guided bombs damaging multi‑storey buildings (Source: @UkraineNow/82217).
Ukrainian UAVs were reported heading toward Zaporizhzhia from the south, indicating ongoing southern aerial operations (Source: @kpszsu/66612, @kpszsu/66556).
Russian civilian fuel shortages and long queues were reported in response to Ukrainian attacks on Russian fuel infrastructure (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31213).
Ukrainian intelligence noted possible use of hypersonic and cruise missiles against southern targets, though no specific strikes were confirmed (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68111).
Russian air defense claimed to have downed 124 Ukrainian UAVs over Russian territory and the Black Sea in a single day (Source: @tass_agency/382692).

Ukrainian drone attacks were reported across multiple fronts, including mass attacks on Okhtyrka district and numerous strikes on fuel stations in Dnipropetrovsk (Source: @UkraineNow/82219, @UkraineNow/82229).
Ukrainian UAVs were observed heading toward strategic locations such as Zaporizhzhia, Kharkiv, and Kyiv, indicating a broad aerial campaign (Source: @kpszsu/66612, @kpszsu/66627, @kpszsu/66629).
SBU conducted a successful strike on the Vtorovo oil‑product pipeline station in Vladimir oblast, demonstrating deep‑strike air capability (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31213).
Ukrainian forces reported use of guided aerial bombs in Zaporizhzhia, causing civilian casualties and infrastructure damage (Source: @UkraineNow/82217).
Russian warnings of possible Iskander‑M and S‑300 ballistic missile launches suggest heightened air‑defense alert levels (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68145, @mon1tor_ua/68115).

key_developments: Russian drone and missile strikes on Naftogaz facilities in Poltava and Kharkiv regions disrupted energy infrastructure (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31211).
Mass drone attack on Okhtyrka district injured 10 civilians and hit fuel stations, showing increased UAV threat (Source: @UkraineNow/82219).
SBU strike on Vtorovo oil‑product pipeline station in Vladimir oblast disrupted Russian fuel logistics (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31213).
Attacks on Zaporizhzhia and Kharkiv AFS caused fires, building damage and loss of equipment (Source: @UkraineNow/82222).
Over 40 attacks in Dnipropetrovsk region resulted in casualties, infrastructure damage and fires (Source: @UkraineNow/82229).
Russian civilians face fuel shortages and long queues due to Ukrainian attacks on Russian fuel supply (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31213).
Estimates suggest up to 16,000 Ukrainian civilians are held in Russian captivity (Source: @UkraineNow/82224).
sections.threat_assessment: Ukrainian UAV operations are expanding across all fronts, targeting energy infrastructure, logistics hubs, and civilian areas, increasing pressure on Russian supply lines. Russian ballistic missile threats, particularly Iskander‑M launches from western regions, raise the risk of high‑impact strikes on Kyiv and other strategic sites. Deep‑strike capabilities demonstrated by SBU attacks on Russian interior facilities indicate a growing Ukrainian ability to disrupt rear‑area assets, while Russian air defenses claim high UAV interception rates, suggesting an intensifying aerial contest.
sections.regional_response: Ukrainian authorities are emphasizing resilience of energy networks, with local experts highlighting communication gaps that hinder protective measures. Russian civil authorities report severe fuel shortages, reflecting the impact of Ukrainian attacks on Russian logistics. International observers note the humanitarian dimension, with thousands of Ukrainian civilians reportedly detained in Russia and ongoing calls for their release.
sections.intelligence_notes: Continued monitoring of UAV flight paths is essential to anticipate target selection and mitigate civilian casualties. The reported SBU strike on Vtorovo suggests Ukrainian forces possess the capability to conduct precise, long‑range operations within Russian territory. Russian warnings of Iskander‑M and S‑300 launches should be integrated into early‑warning systems for Kyiv and western regions. The scale of civilian fuel shortages in Russia may influence domestic stability and affect Russian operational tempo.
